intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, animated state changes over instant updates when tracking health data over time
facade.axisTargeted: Animated continuity vs instant state change
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Design with flowing transitions between dashboard views, data reveals, and metric updates—motion as information
facade.rejectImplies: Favor snappy, zero-lag interactions—instant load, instant feedback, no motion overhead

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable visual language over sharp or clinical aesthetics
facade.axisTargeted: Rounded vs sharp — a foundational look-and-feel dimension
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use curved cards, buttons, and containers; soften the interface to feel supportive rather than sterile
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer crisp, modern geometry; sharp edges, clean lines, and a more minimalist or technical visual style

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, animated state changes over instant updates when tracking health data over time
evidence.implication: Design with flowing transitions between dashboard views, data reveals, and metric updates—motion as information

facade.hypothesis: Users building a health tracker want tactile, analog warmth rather than cold digital precision — muted earth tones, soft shadows, slightly textured surfaces that feel like physical journaling
facade.label: Warm paper texture
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into natural materials aesthetic, cream/tan/rust color palette, soft rounded edges, hand-drawn elements or fabric textures in UI
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean digital modernity — glass, smooth surfaces, high contrast, minimal texture, precision-focused interface over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Personal health dashboards feel more approachable and less clinical when using warm accent colors and soft typography rather than stark grays and sans-serif minimalism
facade.axisTargeted: Emotional tone — clinical precision vs. human warmth
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User values psychological comfort and motivation in tracking; would likely prefer calming color palettes, rounded corners, and narrative framing over pure data efficiency
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes clarity and objectivity in health data; prefers stark, high-contrast interfaces that feel scientifically authoritative and eliminate emotional subtext

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking multiple long-term experiments prefer persistent navigation (sidebar) paired with vertically scrolling experiment cards rather than tabbed switching or grid pagination
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content layout (persistent vs switching, scroll vs pages)
facade.label: Sidebar with scrolling cards
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into sticky left nav with card-based feed for experiments; users want quick access to nav without losing scroll position
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab-based switching or top navigation; users may prefer contained, page-like views for each experiment type

facade.hypothesis: A conversational, encouraging voice in microcopy and notifications ('Hey, great week!' vs 'Data logged') will feel more motivating for long-term health tracking than neutral clinical language.
facade.label: Casual check-in tone
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, supportive personality that celebrates small wins and uses first-person language throughout the interface.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers objective, data-focused tone that stays out of the way and lets numbers speak—clinical or minimal voice is more trustworthy for health data.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users experience health metrics as more meaningful when new data appears gradually rather than refreshing all at once, creating a moment of attention and ritual
facade.axisTargeted: Transition timing and entrance animation—whether data updates arrive as a unified refresh or staggered reveals
facade.label: Numbers fade in
facade.acceptImplies: Design metric cards and chart updates with sequential, timed entrances; treat each data point as an arrival worth noticing rather than bulk swap
facade.rejectImplies: Batch all updates into instant simultaneous refresh; prioritize immediate information density over staged presentation

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
